---
title: Frequently Asked Questions
author: "Anqi Fu, Balasubramanian Narasimhan, Steven Diamond, Stephen Boyd"
---

## Where can I go for help? {#gen-help}

Please open an issue on the [CVXR GitHub
Issues](https://github.com/cvxgrp/CVXR/issues) page. Include a
minimal reproducible example whenever possible.

Since `CVXR` is an R port of
[CVXPY](https://www.cvxpy.org/), the broader CVXPY community can
also be a valuable resource:

- [CVXPY Discussions on GitHub](https://github.com/cvxpy/cvxpy/discussions) — search for similar modeling questions
- [CVXPY Discord](https://discord.gg/4urRQeGBCr) — active community chat for convex optimization questions

Many modeling and DCP questions that arise in `CVXR` have direct
analogs in `CVXPY`, so answers from these forums often translate
directly.

## My problem is not DCP compliant. How do I reformulate it? {#dcp-help}

It is impossible for us to analyze every individual problem and
determine whether it can be reformulated as a DCP problem. We
unfortunately cannot help with reformulating specific problems.

However, there are excellent resources for learning DCP:

- The @BoydVandenberghe:2004 book and its [additional
  exercises](https://stanford.edu/~boyd/cvxbook/) are the definitive
  reference.
- The [DCP rules](https://dcp.stanford.edu/) website provides an
  interactive tool for checking DCP compliance and understanding the
  composition rules.
- The [CVXPY DCP tutorial](https://www.cvxpy.org/tutorial/dcp/index.html)
  explains the rules with many examples — these apply equally to `CVXR`.
- The examples on this site cover a wide range of problems and may
  contain patterns similar to yours.

## I want to report a bug. {#bug}

Please open an issue on the [CVXR GitHub
Issues](https://github.com/cvxgrp/CVXR/issues) page with a
reproducible example.

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
